hook.text: Two children born the same year in the same city: one grows up in a safe suburb, the other in a disinvested block.

buildup.text: Neighborhood shapes exposure to violence, school quality, environmental toxins, and job access from birth.

discovery.text: Raj Chetty's research found that moving to a better neighborhood before age 13 raises lifetime earnings by 31%.

twist.text: But moving is expensive and disruptive. Improving neighborhoods in place may work better than relocating families.

climax.text: Your address at birth is one of the strongest predictors of your adult income, health, and even life expectancy.

punchline.text: Where you grow up shapes who you become. That's the data.

quiz.question: What did Chetty's research show about neighborhoods?
options: Moving to better neighborhoods before age 13 raises earnings
Neighborhood has no effect on adult outcomes
